scope: scientific exploration (harm attribution + minimal-information phase transition); not SOTA
what_we_learned: Offline TTA harms most audited cells (real harm-rate 0.8333).

R0/R1 diagnostics do NOT retrospectively predict TTA harm above the 0.5 majority baseline (R0 bAcc 0.2556, R1 bAcc 0.4222; underpowered, minority n=9) — consistent with the TOS-1 source-only ceiling; NULL result, not identifiability.
R1 target-unlabeled diagnostics do NOT make target gain identifiable (TOS-1/TU-2 stand).

Minimal paired information: harm-sign estimability is a phase transition in k (observed=True, per-shift k {'prior_shift_only': None, 'concept_shift': None, 'support_failure': 256, 'montage_transport_shift': None}); small true gains need more labels, tiny gains stay unresolved — a labeled slice under an iid sampling contract.
Exact counterexamples remain the proof layer; the real-EEG grids illustrate, they do not prove.
what_remains_unknown: Whether these patterns hold on clinical / non-motor-imagery EEG.
Whether stronger TTA baselines reduce the harm rate.
Whether label-free target support/marginal diagnostics can be made reliable.
Whether minimal-paired anchors can be collected cheaply in realistic BCI workflows.
claim_boundary: Oracle target gain is an evaluation label throughout; R0/R1 harm prediction is retrospective, not target-gain identifiability; k>0 slices are labeled slices under an iid sampling contract. No SOTA claim.
